mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls signal expanding labor income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive changes in PAYEMS are followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 1.0% signals an active monetary tightening cycle that raises discount rates and debt-service costs, compressing equity valuations and generating negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.0%, positive 20-day forward returns occur more than 50% of the time, or the mean excess return versus cash is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in retail sales and rising unemployment jointly signal a broad demand collapse that overwhelms idiosyncratic sector performance, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of RSAFS and UNRATE yields a rank correlation with 60-day forward returns below -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the joint signal fails to underperform either single-series signal by at least 50 basis points annually.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion with unemployment below 5.0%, further increases in industrial production hit capacity constraints and raise input costs, causing margin compression that produces negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5.0%, month-over-month increases in INDPRO are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the information coefficient falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1,000 thousand units signal durable construction demand and backward linkages to materials and employment, forecasting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST readings above 1000 are followed by negative 60-day forward returns more than 50% of the time, or the Sharpe ratio of the long signal is below 0.2 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Falling consumer prices combined with a stable federal funds rate increase real interest rates, tightening financial conditions and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through equity valuation compression.
failure_condition: The joint behavior of falling CPIAUCSL and stable FEDFUNDS fails to produce negative 20-day returns in 60% or more of occurrences, or the interaction term rank IC is above -0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production surprise market participants and cause positive 5-day S&P 500 returns as growth expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: Positive changes in INDPRO are followed by negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of observations, or the mean forward return after an increase is not greater than after a decrease (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP above trend reflects aggregate demand momentum that supports top-line revenue growth and earnings revisions, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Above-median GDPC1 growth realizations are not followed by positive 60-day returns in at least 55% of cases, or the rank IC between GDPC1 and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.
